qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: File write endpoint authorizes existing-file overwrite with file.create instead of file.update
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes missing websocket rate limits and message-size throttling in the Go `github.com/pterodactyl/wings` package, while this finding is a Panel authorization bug in `WriteFileContentRequest` and `FileController::write()` for the REST file-write endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in this session identifies the advisory's websocket denial-of-service root cause in Wings.
evidence: The advisory summary and details describe websocket endpoints in `github.com/pterodactyl/wings` with no visible rate limiting and no total message-size limit, leading to CPU, memory, and network exhaustion.

The only accepted final finding in `.judge/input_manifest.json` is `file_write_permission_bypass`, which targets the Panel client file-write path `POST /api/client/servers/{server}/files/write?file=<path>` and a permission mismatch between `file.create` and `file.update`.

The preserved source references for the finding are Panel PHP/TypeScript files under `artifacts/.tmp_panel_upstream` (`app/Http/Requests/Api/Client/Servers/Files/WriteFileContentRequest.php`, `app/Http/Controllers/Api/Client/Servers/FileController.php`, `app/Models/Permission.php`, and `resources/scripts/components/server/files/FileEditContainer.tsx`), not websocket throttling code in Wings.
